status_notes: Initial research from GCP Cloud Functions documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference pass and human review with live GCP environment.
notes: Serverless compute for GCP. Security focus: IAM invocation control, VPC connector, secret management, ingress settings.

match_nodes.description: IAM controls for who can trigger the function

data.description: IAM permissions control which principals can invoke a Cloud Function. For HTTP-triggered functions, the roles/cloudfunctions.invoker role (Gen1) or roles/run.invoker role (Gen2) must be granted. Granting to allUsers makes the function public.

options.name: Public (allUsers)
options.description: Anyone on the internet can invoke. No authentication required.

options.name: All authenticated users
options.description: Any Google account can invoke. Not recommended — very broad.

options.name: Specific principals
options.description: Named users, service accounts, or groups. Least-privilege.

data.vendor_recommendation: Require authentication for all production functions. Never grant allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers unless the function is intentionally public. Use specific service accounts for service-to-service invocation.

match_nodes.description: Connect to VPC for private resource access

data.description: Serverless VPC Access connectors allow Cloud Functions to connect to resources in a VPC network. The function routes traffic through the connector to reach private IPs (Cloud SQL, Memorystore, internal VMs, on-premises via Cloud VPN).
data.vendor_recommendation: Use VPC connectors when functions need access to private resources. Route all egress through VPC for network-level security controls. Use dedicated /28 subnet for the connector.

audit.cli_connectors: gcloud compute networks vpc-access connectors list --region {region}

configure.cli_create_connector: gcloud compute networks vpc-access connectors create {connector} --region {region} --network {vpc} --range 10.8.0.0/28

match_nodes.description: Control where requests can originate from

data.description: Ingress settings restrict the network origin of requests that can reach the function. Controls whether the function accepts traffic from the public internet, only from within the Google Cloud network, or only through a load balancer.

options.name: Allow all traffic
options.description: Default. Function accepts requests from any source.

options.name: Allow internal only
options.description: Only traffic from VPC networks in the same project or VPC SC perimeter.

options.name: Allow internal and GCLB
options.description: Internal traffic plus traffic through Google Cloud Load Balancing.

default.note: Functions accept all traffic by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use ALLOW_INTERNAL_ONLY or ALLOW_INTERNAL_AND_GCLB for production functions. Only use ALLOW_ALL for intentionally public functions.

match_nodes.description: Mount secrets as environment variables or volumes

data.description: Cloud Functions can access secrets from Secret Manager directly. Secrets are mounted as environment variables or file system volumes at function startup. The function's service account must have the secretmanager.secretAccessor role on the secret.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use Secret Manager for all sensitive configuration values. Never hardcode secrets in function code or environment variables. Grant secret access to the function's dedicated service account only.

data.description: Every Cloud Function runs as a service account. This identity determines what Google Cloud APIs and resources the function can access. By default, Gen1 functions use the App Engine default service account and Gen2 functions use the default compute service account — both have broad permissions.

options.name: Default service account
options.description: Broad Editor-level permissions. Discouraged for production.

options.name: Custom service account
options.description: User-created with specific, least-privilege IAM roles.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use a dedicated service account with minimal permissions for each function. Never use the default compute or App Engine service account in production.

match_nodes.description: Customer-managed encryption keys for function code and config

data.description: Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) allow you to use Cloud KMS keys to encrypt function source code, build artifacts, and configuration data at rest. Without CMEK, Google manages the encryption keys.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use CMEK for functions processing sensitive data where your organization requires key management control. Ensure key access is not accidentally revoked, which would break function deployment.
